summary: The reproduction mounts the vulnerable FUXA `v1.2.10` source tree, initializes the real users runtime and admin-only users API, and confirms that a caller without any token can list users and create a new admin user while `secureEnabled=false`.
expected_security_impact: An unauthenticated caller should be able to reach an admin-only API route, read the existing user list, and create a new admin user without providing any JWT or API key.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned FUXA `v1.2.10` at `b873cdf32a63bcfaa6de8b724781f63c6f977c69`, built the Docker image, mounted the cloned repository into the container, and printed `EVOHUNT:UNAUTH_GET_STATUS=200`, `EVOHUNT:UNAUTH_POST_STATUS=200`, `EVOHUNT:INITIAL_USERS=admin`, `EVOHUNT:FINAL_USERS=admin,attacker`, and `EVOHUNT:SUCCESS unauthenticated caller listed users and created an admin user via /api/users while secureEnabled=false`.
verification_method: The Node.js harness uses the repository's own `verify-api-or-token` middleware, `users` API route, and `runtime/users` storage module. It verifies that an unauthenticated GET returns the seeded admin row with a bcrypt password hash and that an unauthenticated POST creates a second user with admin group `-1`.
evidence_produced_by_run: EVOHUNT:SUCCESS unauthenticated caller listed users and created an admin user via /api/users while secureEnabled=false

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host at tag `v1.2.10`, verifies commit `b873cdf32a63bcfaa6de8b724781f63c6f977c69`, and mounts that checkout into the container at `/target-repo`.
